codeReview.summary: The fix addresses the prior blocker: it adds explicit VAL-CROSS-039 concurrent cross-flow coverage and hardens persistence paths by syncing users before first pantry/recipe writes plus revalidating affected routes after create/delete mutations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture guidance is currently high-level on user sync and does not explicitly call out that first-write server actions touching user-foreign-key tables should invoke `syncUser()` before inserts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:21 describes sync on the first authenticated request; this fix had to add `syncUser()` in src/actions/add-pantry-item.ts:21, src/actions/save-recipe.ts:11, and src/actions/save-converted-recipe.ts:13.

summary: Re-review passed. The previous failure (missing explicit concurrent no-corruption verification) is now covered by src/__tests__/cross-flow-concurrent-usage.test.tsx:110-159 with pantry add -> generated+converted recipe coexistence -> pantry integrity checks, and related persistence hardening is reflected in sync/revalidation action changes and updated tests.
